source_pr.title: test: try to stable some tests
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: Try to close #19636 

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:
Increase the default `managedLockTTL` for these tests and restore after finish.

How it Works:

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test


Side effects


### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- Try to stabilize some tests.

symptoms: Pessimistic-lock related tests can be flaky when the default managed-lock TTL is too small under CI load (lock expiry / lock wait races).
failure_signature: Intermittent lock wait/nowait assertions fail due to managed lock TTL expiring too quickly under load
root_cause_explanation: These pessimistic-lock tests depend on a managed-lock TTL (`tikv.ManagedLockTTL`). With a small default TTL, locks can expire or behave differently under slow/loaded CI, causing intermittent lock wait/nowait assertions to fail. This PR stabilizes the tests by increasing `tikv.ManagedLockTTL` to a larger value within the test scope (and restoring it afterward), widening the timing window for lock-related expectations.
fix_pattern: Increase the managed lock TTL for timing-sensitive pessimistic-lock tests (with save/restore) to avoid lock-expiry-related flakes under CI load.

evidence: evidence: PR title: test: try to stable some tests
evidence: set `tikv.ManagedLockTTL` to 3000 (and restore) inside tests to avoid lock expiry / lock wait flakes (session/pessimistic_test.go)
